name: chDB
description: chDB is an in-process SQL OLAP engine powered by ClickHouse. It embeds the full ClickHouse query engine directly into Python, enabling fast analytical queries on Parquet, CSV, JSON, Arrow, and 70+ other formats without a server. With zero external dependencies, minimized data copies via Python memoryview, and a pandas-compatible DataStore API supporting 630+ methods, chDB delivers up to 20x speedup over pandas for grouped aggregations and complex pipelines.
format_on_disk: ClickHouse MergeTree columnar format (persistent or in-memory)
core_strengths: Full ClickHouse SQL engine embedded in-process with zero external dependencies
Direct querying of Parquet, CSV, JSON, Arrow, ORC and 70+ formats
pandas-compatible DataStore API with 630+ methods and SQL optimization
Minimized data copies from C++ to Python via memoryview
Columnar vectorized execution for fast OLAP aggregations and joins
Up to 20x faster than pandas on common grouped aggregation operations
best_use_cases: Accelerating pandas workflows with a drop-in DataStore API replacement
In-process analytical queries on local Parquet/CSV/Arrow files
Embedded OLAP inside data science notebooks and Python scripts
Data pipeline transformations with SQL and 70+ I/O formats
Ad-hoc analysis on datasets too large for pandas but not needing a server
remarks: chDB is Python-only for the bundled bindings; Go, Rust, Node.js, Bun, and C/C++ bindings are available separately. The DataStore API provides a pandas-compatible interface but not all pandas methods are supported. Performance is best on analytical (OLAP) workloads, not transactional (OLTP) patterns.
